source.principle: WordPress core parses theme headers via get_file_data() which reads the first 8 KiB of the file and applies regex /^(?:[ \t]*<\?php)?[ \t/*#@]*<Field>:(.*)$/mi for each declared header. 'Version' is one of the declared headers in WP_Theme::$file_headers and is required by the WordPress.org Theme Repository. Slug charset widened to [^/?#]+ to match actual WP behavior (filesystem directory names allow dots, uppercase, etc; e.g., Newspaper12.7.3-child, vidi_v1.0 in real corpus). Per src/wp-includes/class-wp-theme.php WP does no validation on the slug -- it is whatever directory exists.
source.doc_quote_required_fields: Version: The version of the theme, written in X.X or X.X.X format. (Required)

slug_url_rules.name_prefix_rationale: Theme slugs collide with plugin slugs (e.g. 'astra' is both a popular theme and several plugins). Prefixing with 'wp-theme:' makes the canonical name unambiguous at reconcile time.
